title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Global aluminum prices fell to $3,576.21 per ton, pressured by rising Chinese inventories and restored vessel flows through the Strait of Hormuz. Prices edged up 0.10% to $3,596.04/ton amid low LME inventories and Strait of Hormuz tensions, but easing geopolitical risks capped gains. China's production is constrained near its 45-million-ton capacity ahead of Labour Day holiday.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3538.50 USD/T on April 29, 2026, down 0.03% from the previous day, though up 3.12% over the past month. UK aluminum futures held above $3,550 per tonne near a four-year high due to Strait of Hormuz blockage threatening Middle Eastern supply. Later, prices rose to 3,553.50 USD/T, up 0.40%, with forecasts at 3643.69 USD/T by quarter-end.

title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
summary: The United States faces a severe aluminum supply crisis, with Bloomberg warning that supplies could hit zero within 30 days due to a 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum. This tariff, intended to protect domestic production, has triggered disruptions threatening U.S. manufacturing. Production issues in the Middle East and a potential deficit of 1.5 million tons exacerbate the shortage.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum, a major U.S. producer, has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs. Fox News senior correspondent Steve Harrigan reports on this development in the context of ongoing trade policies affecting the aluminum industry.

title: Aluminium Up 12%, Nickel Hits 22-Month High, Copper Climbs
summary: Aluminum prices have risen 12% this month amid broader metals market gains, with nickel hitting a 22-month high and copper climbing. This surge reflects strong demand and supply constraints in the commodities sector.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,154 CNY/T on April 30, 2026, up 0.29% from the previous day, with a monthly increase of 0.93% and 2.57% year-over-year. On April 29, prices reached 3,145 CNY/T, supported by strong seasonal demand, restocking, higher domestic delivery prices due to input costs from Middle East conflict, and positive World Steel Association projections for global demand growth.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel increased to 1,127.15 USD/T on April 29, 2026, up 1.73% from the previous day, reaching the highest level since January 2024. Over the past four weeks, prices gained 4.99%.

title: AISI: Raw steel production holds near multi-year high
summary: Domestic raw steel production in the US was estimated at 1,830,000 short tons for the week ending April 25, down 1.0% from the prior week but near the multi-year high of 1,848,000 short tons set the previous week. Year-to-date production totals 29,549,000 short tons, up 6.0% from 2025, with a capability utilization rate of 79.3%. Production decreased week-over-week in four of five regions.

title: Industry Data
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,830,000 net tons in the week ending April 25, 2026. The capability utilization rate stood at 79.3 percent for that week.

title: Copper fell to 5.87 USD/Lbs on April 29, 2026
summary: Copper prices fell to 5.87 USD/Lbs on April 29, 2026, down 0.76% from the previous day, though up 7.24% over the past month and 28.38% over the year. Copper futures steadied around $5.95 per pound after four consecutive sessions of decline, supported by long-term fundamentals despite near-term pressures from the Iran conflict. Earlier, prices hovered around $6 per pound amid stalled US-Iran peace efforts and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent operational challenges at the facility.

title: Ivanhoe Mines Reports 71,417 Tonnes of Copper in Anode Produced by Kamoa-Kakula in Q1 2026; Recovery Efforts Advancing
summary: Ivanhoe Mines reported production of 71,417 tonnes of copper in anode at the Kamoa-Kakula complex in Q1 2026, with Africa's largest copper smelter ramping up to 60% capacity. Sales of 99.7%-pure anodes and high-strength sulphuric acid are improving amid global supply chain disruptions. Revised guidance outlines ramp-up to over 500,000 tonnes of copper per annum from 2028 onwards.

title: Capstone Copper Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
summary: Capstone Copper reported Q1 2026 copper production of 19,018 tonnes, 16% lower than Q1 2025, due to reduced copper concentrate and cathode output. Consolidated production totaled 47,960 tonnes, down 11% from 53,796 tonnes in Q1 2025, primarily from lower sulphide operations. The company highlighted ongoing operational challenges affecting capacity.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices fell to 19,210 USD/T on April 29, 2026, down 1.13% from the previous day, despite a 10.88% monthly rise. Futures traded above $19,450, a near two-year high, driven by supply disruptions including Indonesia's mining quota cuts and a sulfur shortage from Strait of Hormuz issues. These factors have constrained ore supply and raised processing costs for nickel intermediates.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Nickel prices edged up 0.19% to $19,127.31/Ton globally and ¥130,526/Ton in China. Bullish momentum stems from supply constraints, including Indonesia's slashed mining quotas tightening ore availability and a global sulfur shortage due to Middle East tensions impacting MHP processing. A weaker US dollar and firm SHFE supplier quotes further support prices.

title: Nickel Enters a New Era as Indonesia Tightens Supply and Prices Surge
summary: The global nickel market is shifting to a tightly managed pricing paradigm due to tightening supply and rising input costs, with prices surging to $19,200 per ton toward a $20,000-$21,000 target. Indonesia's calibrated strategy of mining quota reductions aims to stabilize prices in this range. The market has broken out of the $17,000-$18,000 range, now trading around $19,200.
